questions.question: Explain the working of HTTP with a neat diagram.
keyPoints: HTTP is a request-response protocol between client and server
Client sends HTTP request with method, URL, headers
Server processes request and sends HTTP response with status, headers, body
Communication happens over TCP/IP connection

questions.question: List and explain four HTTP methods with their purposes.
keyPoints: GET - Retrieve data from server
POST - Submit data to be processed
PUT - Update existing resource
DELETE - Remove resource from server

questions.question: What is a URL? Explain its components with an example.
keyPoints: URL is Uniform Resource Locator
Components: protocol (http/https), hostname (www.example.com), port (optional), path (/page), query string (?id=1), fragment (#section)
Example: https://www.example.com:443/index.html?id=10#section1

questions.question: Explain the different categories of HTTP status codes.
keyPoints: 1xx - Informational: Request received, processing
2xx - Success: Request successfully received and accepted
3xx - Redirection: Further action needed to complete request
4xx - Client Error: Bad syntax or cannot fulfill
5xx - Server Error: Server failed to fulfill valid request

questions.question: What are cookies? Explain their role in HTTP communication.
keyPoints: Cookies are small pieces of data stored on client side
Server sends Set-Cookie header in response
Browser stores cookie and sends Cookie header in subsequent requests
Used for session tracking, user preferences, authentication

questions.question: Differentiate between HTTP/1.0 and HTTP/1.1.
keyPoints: HTTP/1.0: New connection for each request, no caching support
HTTP/1.1: Persistent connections (keep-alive), virtual hosting support
HTTP/1.1: Chunked transfer encoding, better caching with conditional GET
HTTP/1.1: Introduced host header for virtual hosting

questions.question: Explain the architecture of World Wide Web with a diagram. Discuss the roles of web client, web server, and proxy server.
keyPoints: Architecture: Client ↔ Internet ↔ Server with HTTP protocol
Web Client (Browser): Sends requests, renders HTML, manages cookies
Web Server: Receives requests, processes them, sends responses with resources
Proxy Server: Acts as intermediary, provides caching, filtering, anonymity
HTTP operates on client-server model using request-response cycle

questions.question: Explain the HTTP request and response message formats. Give examples of headers in each.
keyPoints: Request format: Request line, Headers, Empty line, Body
Example: GET /index.html HTTP/1.1, Host: example.com
Response format: Status line, Headers, Empty line, Body
Example: HTTP/1.1 200 OK, Content-Type: text/html
Headers: General, Request, Response, Entity headers

questions.question: What is HTTPS? Explain its working and benefits over HTTP.
keyPoints: HTTPS = HTTP over SSL/TLS (Secure Sockets Layer/Transport Layer Security)
Uses asymmetric cryptography for key exchange, symmetric for data encryption
Default port 443 vs HTTP port 80
Benefits: Authentication, Data Integrity, Confidentiality, SEO advantage
Certificate-based authentication of server identity

questions.question: Explain web caching mechanism and its advantages. Discuss different caching techniques in HTTP.
keyPoints: Web caching stores copies of responses at various network points
Reduces bandwidth, server load, latency for users
Browser cache: Local storage in user browser
Proxy cache: Shared cache at ISP/network level
Server cache: Application-level caching at origin server
HTTP caching headers: Cache-Control, Expires, ETag, Last-Modified, If-None-Match
